title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Two-cornered contest in Vepanahalli constituency
content: Vepanahalli is primarily agrarian with horticultural cropping. Parts of Shoolagiri and Udhanapalli are facing imminent land alienation with notification of lands for establishment of SIPCOT industrial estates, given its proximity to Hosur’s industrial ecosystem.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Gaps in road infrastructure, solid waste management core issues in Tiruppur South constituency
content: The major industrial hub is struggling to cater to the infrastructure needs, particularly adequacy of roads.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Ponneri constituency residents yet to reap benefits of industrial development
content: They demand that a government women’s college be constructed either in Ponneri or Minjur as students have to go to colleges in and around Chennai city for higher education; residents also want Ponneri Taluk to be carved out from Tiruvallur district and be made a separate district

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Will Panneerselvam turn over a new leaf under the ‘Rising Sun’ in Bodinayakkanur constituency?
content: While his supporters are at pains to highlight the circumstances that compelled him to switch to the DMK from the AIADMK, the ruling party workers are yet to embrace him in a big way. He is also facing criticism in his native Theni district for not bringing in big infrastructure projects

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Coimbatore North constituency voters look for wider roads, support for MSMEs
content: According to Ashok, a senior citizen residing at Vadavalli, though his neighbourhood has 24*7 drinking water connection, water supply is so irregular that no one knows when and for long the supply will be.

title: Vandalism of Ambedkar statue in Ghaziabad sparks outrage
content: A SWAT team has also reached the spot and is working on the matter. CCTV footage from the area is being examined. Strict action will be taken against those involved, says DCP, Rural Ghaziabad, Surendra Nath Tiwari.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Effluent discharge a major issue in Veerapandi constituency
content: The constituency primarily houses farmers cultivating millets, sugarcane, flowers, and vegetables. The main support for farming is the Thirumanimutharu, a river in which effluents are discharged from illegal dyeing units, are flowing in this river. forcing farmers to use groundwater and other water sources.

title: Temperature breaches 40°C in 5 districts
content: According to data from the India Meteorological Department (IMD), four districts in north interior Karnataka and one district in south interior Karnataka saw the mercury rising above the 40°C mark.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Alangulam constituency lacks job opportunities, vegetable storage facilities
content: Even though literacy rate in this constituency has gone up considerably over the years and the educationally qualified youth are leaving for other areas for employment, their parents are still languishing in beedi rolling industry

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Despite a slew of infrastructure projects, Poonamallee constituency residents point to gaps in civic amenities
content: They demand a functional underground sewer system, motorable interior roads, and reliable drinking water supply; despite housing the Chembarambakkam lake, the area continues to face water supply issues; DMK MLA Krishnaswamy says most poll promises have been met

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Fishermen in Colachel constituency seek fair representation
content: Colachel, one of the municipalities predominantly occupied by fishermen population, has a great cultural and historic heritage.

title: AIADMK looks to defend Melur and Usilampatti, its southern strongholds
content: Intra-party conflicts, which eroded the party’s core base in the southern districts, do not have much impact on these two constituencies in Madurai; while the AIADMK has won from the Melur Assembly segment five times consecutively since 2001, Usilampatti has rarely elected a DMK candidate

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Thiruvadanai constituency faces water scarcity, administrative limbo
content: Local farmers and residents have highlighted a persistent need for more Direct Procurement Centres (DPCs) to streamline the sale of their produce.

title: Export disruption hits ‘Bangalore Rose’ onion farmers; MP seeks urgent support
content: In a letter to Union Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan, MP for Chickballapur Sudhakar has urged the Centre to step in with immediate market support measures. He has called for procurement interventions to prevent distress in sales and stabilise prices.

title: Bengaluru: Phase-2 elevated corridors likely to be delayed by two years
content: The two flyovers that will be delayed are the Nagawara Junction to Bagaluru Main Road, spanning 17.30 km, and the Mother Teresa Circle (Ulsoor Lake Junction) to Bagaluru Village stretch, spanning about 27.20 km.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Development, welfare, civic problems shape poll mood in Thiru. Vi. Ka. Nagar constituency
content: Residents say their struggle for basic amenities in some areas still persist; night food stalls and commercial activity have encroached road space and worsened traffic congestion; those living in railway lands in Rajiv Gandhi Nagar and Mangalapuram are also seeking alternate housing

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Ambattur constituency residents demand GH, sewer network, wider roads
content: Residents want an additional platform on the southern side of the Ambattur railway station for express and fast local trains towards Chennai to halt

title: Diplomats’ families and students fleeing Iran return to India
content: Amidst tenuous ceasefire between Iran and U.S., groups undertake gruelling journeys to cross over into Azerbaijan and Armenia

title: Linking women’s reservation with delimitation is unconstitutional, contends G. Ramakrishnan
content: He says by doing this, the Centre knew full well that opposition parties will oppose the Bill, and once they did, it can campaign against the opposition in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu based on the issue

title: National Herald case: Delhi High Court to hear on Monday ED plea against Sonia, Rahul
content: Besides the Gandhis, the High Court had also issued notices to Suman Dubey, Sam Pitroda, Young Indian, Dotex Merchandise Pvt. Ltd. and Sunil Bhandari on the ED’s plea

title: Five deaths, no arrests as fresh violence grips Manipur
content: As the attackers remain unidentified, locals have hit the streets multiple times in the past one week and have clashed with security forces demanding arrests

title: Police suspect loan app operators in youth’s disappearance in Vadakara
content: Police say the search for the 25-year-old youth is in full swing, with support from the hi-tech crime inquiry cell

title: Call to ensure quality of food sold at Kozhikode beach stalls
content: Members of various residents’ associations, senior citizens’ groups and tourism entrepreneurs demand a permanent mechanism to monitor food quality

title: DMK and its allies shattered women’s dreams, says Edappadi K. Palaniswami
content: “The DMK alliance does not want to give reservation for women,” the AIADMK general secreatary Palaniswami charged

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Karur constituency, a hub of export-oriented textile units and bus building facilities
content: Karur faces degradation of environment due to industrialisation and urbanisation; drinking water scarcity has been constant complaint of the people against the successive governments

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Mudukulathur constituency expects its rightful share of Vaigai waters
content: Though not wholly fed by the Vaigai river, the farming carried out here, relying predominantly on a complex network of rain-fed tanks and seasonal canal, occupies a significant part of the local economy.

title: 23 killed in explosion at fireworks unit near Virudhunagar
content: Most victims charred beyond recognition, with many bodies blown away by the impact of the explosion; the fireworks industry is shut on Sundays, but the unit was operating without permission, Virudhunagar Collector N.O. Sukhaputra

title: Mamata betrayed Bengal's women by conspiring with parties like Congress: Nitin Nabin
content: Urging women voters in the State to "teach her a lesson", he claimed that the Trinamool government had failed to ensure the safety and security of women

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Tiruchi East constituency is grappling with civic issues for long
content: With TVK leader C. Joseph Vijay choosing Tiruchi East as one of the two constituencies to make his electoral debut, the spotlight has turned to the densely populated Assembly segment spread around the city’s most famous landmark: Rockfort.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: In Palacode constituency, two-cornered contest on the cards
content: The potential for agro-based industries remain untapped despite demand from farmers for value-addition for the tomatoes and mangoes grown in Palacode and copra production centre for the copra cultivation in Marandahalli.

title: Dagadarthi airport set to be game-changer for Nellore, Prakasam districts, says MP
content: District Collector invites private participants to submit Request For Proposals by the end of April 2026

title: Two U.S. citizens detained at Srinagar airport over satellite-based GPS device
content: The police, during searches, spotted the Garmin GPS device, which can be operated from a satellite. Thereafter, two U.S. citizens were deboarded and the device was seized

title: PM Modi’s jhalmuri break in Jhargram leaves Trinamool fuming
content: Jharkhand Chief Minister Hemant Soren and his wife Kalpana Soren had to return after waiting for hours to campaign in Jhargram as they were not allowed to land, the party says

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: R.K. Nagar constituency residents still await safe drinking water
content: The narrow arterial roads, congested bylanes, closely built row houses and busy market areas - all hallmarks of a north Chennai locality - still define the area, even as the introduction of metro rail connectivity has noticeably changed the area’s landscape.

title: Modi says Trinamool prevented passage of women’s reservation Bill; Mamata reminds him it was passed in 2023
content: The Prime Minister addresses four public meetings in Bankura, Purulia, Jhargram and Paschim Medinipur and calls the Trinamool ‘anti-tribal’. The Chief Minister hits back, citing raids at Kolkata police officer in charge of her security and asks whether there is a plan to eliminate her

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Radhapuram constituency set to witness electoral battle of Appavu again
content: Radhapuram has to find solutions to a lot more problems like increased bus services to Radhapuram from the villages like Ittamozhi in the north, Thalapathi Samudram in the west and Levingipuram in the south.

title: Bengals extend Lawrence after blockbuster trade
content: Defensive tackle Dexter Lawrence II passed his physical with the Bengals and has signed a one-year, $28 million extension, the Win Sports Group told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: NFL draft prospect Branch arrested in Georgia
content: Wide receiver Zachariah Branch, who is widely expected to be a Day 2 NFL draft pick, was arrested early Sunday morning in Athens, Georgia, on two possible misdemeanor charges.

title: Arteta: EPL 'starts again' after loss to Man City
content: Mikel Arteta has suggested "the Premier League starts again" after Arsenal were beaten at Manchester City on Sunday but insisted the Gunners still believe they can win the title.

title: Barkley: Jordan rift easing, golf game planned
content: Charles Barkley says he and Michael Jordan are talking again after nearly 15 years and that the two plan to play golf together in the offseason.

title: Homa, after criticizing golf outbursts, throws club
content: Days after saying on-course outbursts cast a bad look on pro golfers, Max Homa threw his club in frustration during the final round of the RBC Heritage.

title: Kane scores as Bayern retains Bundesliga title
content: Bayern Munich have sealed their defence of the Bundesliga title after thrashing Stuttgart 4-2 on Sunday.

title: Perez, Quatraro clear air about 'mental breather'
content: Salvador Perez and Royals manager Matt Quatraro said they were back on the same page Sunday after the catcher's social media post on Saturday generated drama.

title: 🏈 Kiper: Favorites from this year's NFL draft class
content: Mel Kiper Jr.'s annual list of NFL draft favorites includes two speedy receivers, three undersized cornerbacks and some gritty linemen.

title: Stanley Cup playoffs daily: What to watch in Sunday's four Game 1s
content: Eight new teams open their postseason Sunday. Here's what to watch, along with Saturday's highlights and stats.

title: 'Nobody was going to beat the 10th pick:' How the Bengals wooed the Giants into trading vet Dexter Lawrence
content: Lawrence informed the Giants he wanted a new deal or trade, the Giants didn't want to let him go -- until the price was right.

title: At a crucial stage in the season, Man City take control of the title race over Arsenal
content: Manchester City's 2-1 win over Arsenal becomes a huge confidence boost for Pep Guardiola's side in the Premier League title race.

title: Van Dijk hands Liverpool a UCL boost and reason to believe after more late drama
content: Liverpool won with a late goal to go seven points clear of Chelsea in the race for the Champions League.

title: Postseason preview: Matchups, biggest questions and what to know
content: Everything you need to know for the Thunder, Pistons, Spurs, Celtics and 16 other teams chasing the 2026 NBA title.

title: Offseason guides: Draft, free agency, trade targets for eliminated teams
content: Summer is here for franchises that missed the playoffs. What lies ahead for the Hornets, Clippers and more?

title: Why Jimmy Johnson cautions Jets that making right picks more valuable than stockpiling them
content: With eight picks in the first and second rounds, New York sits where the former Cowboys coach was 35 years ago.

title: Inside the fallout of the Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel photos
content: How Dianna Russini and New England Patriots coach Mike Vrabel scrambled to respond to photos published in the New York Post.

title: The biggest question facing every WNBA team in training camp
content: How will Angel Reese fit the Dream? Will the Wings return to the playoffs? Can Caitlin Clark remain healthy?

title: Playoff concessions go big: NBA, NHL teams unveil new foods
content: It's playoff time in the NBA and NHL, and some teams are breaking out some wild concessions for fans to enjoy in the postseason.

title: Draft guide: Rankings, strategies and more
content: Everything you need to know before your fantasy women's basketball draft, including rankings, tips and news.

title: 10 NFL draft prospects to put on your fantasy radar -- and three others to monitor
content: With the NFL draft nearly upon us, it's time to think about how the top prospects will fare in fantasy.

title: Transfer rumors, news: PSG move for Arsenal star Martinelli
content: Paris Saint-Germain sporting director Luis Campos has held a meeting with the representatives of Arsenal winger Gabriel Martinelli. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Talks, threats, and turbulence: Trump sends team back to Pak as defiant Iran stares him down
content: A US delegation heads to Pakistan for crucial talks with Iran, despite President Trump's escalating threats to cripple Iran's infrastructure if a deal isn't reached. Tehran remains hesitant, demanding the lifting of sanctions and unblocking of assets. Trump's aggressive rhetoric, aimed at forcing negotiations, faces domestic criticism for its potential to escalate conflict.

title: Delimitation decoded: What the Centre tried to do, why oppn shot it down & what happens next
content: A government bill linking women's reservation to a landmark seat expansion in the parliament and state assemblies was defeated on the floor of the Lok Sabha. The opposition called it a victory against delimitation "by the backdoor." Here is what the bill sought to do, why it collapsed, and what the constitutional fight over representation will look like next.

title: Arya and Connolly’s brutal ball-striking tears apart LSG as PBKS seal 54-run win
content: Priyansh Arya and Cooper Connolly delivered a stunning 182-run partnership off 80 balls, propelling Punjab Kings to a formidable 254/7. Their elegant yet brutal hitting, characterized by precise timing rather than brute force, dismantled the Lucknow Super Giants' bowling attack. This exceptional display set the stage for a dominant 54-run victory.

title: When Palantir CEO says working at billion dollar company better than degree from Harvard
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp declared that working at the company is a superior credential to degrees from elite universities. He emphasized that Palantir's environment makes prior educational backgrounds irrelevant, positioning the company itself as the ultimate career asset.

title: OpenAI exec Srinivas Narayanan leaves to spend time with ageing parents in India
content: Srinivas Narayanan, OpenAI's CTO for B2B applications, is departing the company next week after nearly three years. He plans to visit his parents in India before considering future professional endeavors. Narayanan, who previously served as VP of Engineering, shared his departure on LinkedIn, calling his tenure at the AI firm 'three incredible years'.

title: Tanvi Kolte wins Bigg Boss Marathi 6; Raqesh Bapat is runner-up
content: Tanvi Kolte emerged as the winner of Bigg Boss Marathi 6, lifting the coveted trophy after an intense season filled with drama, emotions, and fierce competition. Raqesh Bapat secured the position of first runner-up, winning hearts with his composed gameplay. The grand finale celebrated the contestants’ journeys, marking the end of a memorable and highly successful season.

title: Priyansh Arya & Connolly script history; break Gilchrist, Shaun Marsh's record to...
content: This is the third 150-plus stand for Punjab Kings in the tournament. The highest remains the 206-run partnership between Adam Gilchrist and Shaun Marsh against Royal Challengers Bengaluru in 2011. It is followed by the 183-run stand between KL Rahul and Mayank Agarwal against Rajasthan Royals in 2020.

title: Yoshua Bengio reveals the problem with Anthropic on not releasing Mythos to public
content: AI pioneer Yoshua Bengio criticizes Anthropic's restricted release of its powerful 'Claude Mythos' AI, capable of finding zero-day vulnerabilities. He argues that concentrating such critical cybersecurity decision-making in one private company, primarily US-based, risks leaving global infrastructure unprotected. Bengio calls for international oversight to ensure fair access and prevent misuse.

title: Josh Allen, Hailee Steinfeld step out glowing after welcoming their first child
content: NFL star Josh Allen and actress Hailee Steinfeld welcomed their baby daughter, sharing the joyous news on Thursday. Just a day later, the new parents made a dazzling appearance at Josh's best friend Sam Darnold's wedding. The couple, radiating happiness, celebrated with fellow NFL players, even sharing baby photos, proving they can balance new parenthood with social commitments.

title: Aryna Sabalenka’s fiancé Georgios Frangulis hits back at critics over match presence
content: Georgios Frangulis responded strongly to critics questioning his presence at Aryna Sabalenka’s matches. In a podcast video, he explained how he balances work and support for his fiancé. Sabalenka also shared how the relationship has shaped her mindset. Meanwhile, she continues a strong tennis season despite a recent injury forcing her to miss the Stuttgart Open.

title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce plan wedding move amid security concerns
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are gearing up to get married after being together for close to three years now. While Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have been rumored to be tying the knot on 3rd July in New York City, details have been kept private. Amid all this, it seems the high profile couple is focused on giving back to society.

title: IPL: Riyan Parag under fire as horror run continues with another flop vs KKR
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag's batting struggles persisted in IPL 2026, as he managed only 12 runs against Kolkata Knight Riders.  Across six matches, Parag has scored a mere 61 runs, highlighting a worrying dip in form. His dismissal contributed to a middle-order collapse, leaving Royals with a below-par total.

title: Waiting for CBSE class 12 result? The last 5 years reveal patterns too clear to ignore
content: As students wait for the CBSE Class 12 Result 2026, the last five comparable years point to a clear trend. Pass percentages have steadied, girls have stayed ahead, southern regions keep leading, and the top-score pool looks less inflated than before. The result, when it comes, may do more than declare marks. It may confirm a pattern already in plain sight.

title: Pak says it will repay remaining $1.5 bn loan to UAE by April 23 amid IMF funding hopes
content: Pakistan aims to repay the remaining $1.5 billion loan to the UAE by April 23, following a $2 billion repayment. This comes as the country anticipates a $1.2 billion disbursement from the IMF after recent negotiations. Saudi Arabia has also extended a $3 billion deposit maturity, depositing $2 billion to bolster reserves.

title: What actually happens in your body 30 minutes after eating sugar
content: Within 30 minutes of consuming sugar, your body experiences a rapid glucose surge, triggering insulin release. This can lead to a temporary pleasure hit followed by an energy crash, creating a cycle of cravings. Experts advise moderating sugary foods and increasing protein and fiber to manage blood sugar and long-term health.

title: 'Will knock out every power plant’: Trump warns Iran as US delegation heads to Pak
content: A US delegation is heading to Islamabad for fresh talks with Iran after previous negotiations faltered. President Trump issued a stern warning, vowing severe action if Iran rejects the "fair and reasonable" deal offered. Tensions remain high following disagreements over Iran's nuclear program and sanctions, with the Strait of Hormuz becoming a focal point of the dispute

title: Rahane's duck tale gets longer, KKR captain tops unwanted list as poor form continues
content: Chasing 156, Rahane was dismissed early, adding to his struggles this season. He had also been out for a golden duck in KKR’s previous match against Gujarat Titans.

title: India’s clean energy push: Govt mulls bids for 220 MWe Small Modular Reactor
content: India is poised to advance its nuclear energy program by inviting bids for the 220 MWe Bharat Small Modular Reactor (BSMR-200) within six months. This initiative, a key part of the nation's clean energy transition, will allow foreign companies to partner with local firms.

title: ‘Luck was on my side’: Rinku Singh reveals game-changing moment in KKR win over RR
content: Kolkata Knight Riders secured their first IPL 2026 victory with a thrilling four-wicket win over Rajasthan Royals, thanks to Rinku Singh's unbeaten 53. Singh admitted a dropped catch early in his innings proved to be the turning point, allowing him to guide KKR through a nervy chase to the finish line.

title: Deepika expecting 2nd baby with Ranveer, makes announcement via social media
content: Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh are expecting their second child, with Deepika sharing a cute social media post hinting at her baby bump. The couple, married in 2018 in Italy, already welcomed daughter Dua in 2024, and fans are thrilled as their family grows once again.

title: IPL: KKR snap losing streak as Rinku Singh holds nerve in RR last-over thriller
content: Kolkata Knight Riders ended their six-match winless streak in IPL 2026, securing a four-wicket victory against Rajasthan Royals at the Eden Gardens on Sunday. Rinku Singh's unbeaten 53 guided KKR to the target with two balls to spare, a much-needed win after repeated collapses. The win lifts them off the bottom of the table.

title: 'Do you eat onions?' PM Modi takes 'jhalmuri break' during Bengal poll campaign - watch
content: Amidst a high-stakes political campaign in West Bengal, Prime Minister Narendra Modi took a refreshing pause for a jhalmuri snack in Jhargram. He interacted with the vendor, inquiring about the price and confirming his liking for onions with a witty remark. This candid moment unfolded as the Prime Minister intensified his campaign efforts across the state.

title: Maharashtra couple fight over half-cooked mutton, woman later kills herself
content: A Yavatmal homemaker died after consuming poison. Chetana Jadhav reportedly took the extreme step following an argument with her husband over undercooked mutton. The dispute occurred in front of a guest. Chetana felt humiliated and consumed poison later. She was rushed to the hospital but declared dead. Police have registered a case against her husband.

title: No fuel shortage: Govt assures 100% domestic LPG, PNG, CNG supply amid energy crunch
content: Amidst geopolitical tensions, the government assures uninterrupted fuel availability, with no disruption in LPG supply and normal deliveries. Citizens are urged to avoid panic buying as efforts are underway to ensure petrol, diesel, and LPG availability. Indian shipping continues to navigate the Middle East safely, with authorities closely monitoring the situation and ensuring the safety of all seafarers.

title: Jofra Archer scripts history vs KKR, becomes only second bowler to...
content: Opening the bowling for Rajasthan Royals, Archer bowled KKR opener Tim Seifert for a golden duck with the first delivery of KKR’s innings. With this, Archer now has five wickets on the first ball of an IPL innings, matching Shami’s record.

title: Tamil Nadu blast: 18 workers killed in Virudhunagar firecracker factory
content: A devastating blast at a firecracker factory in Tamil Nadu's Virudhunagar on Sunday claimed 18 lives and injured six. Chief Minister MK Stalin expressed deep sorrow, extended condolences to the bereaved families, and dispatched ministers to oversee rescue efforts and provide immediate assistance.

title: 'Not in lifetime': Lalit Modi vs Pietersen explodes over The Hundred's IPL comparison
content: The exchange follows Modi’s earlier remarks where he said The Hundred would not survive beyond three years. Pietersen disagreed with that view and said the competition would continue, possibly in a T20 format.

title: Jaipur nikah, Kashmir trail: How LeT's 'Khargosh' managed to flee India
content: A top Lashkar-e-Taiba operative, Umer Harris alias “Khargosh,” has reportedly fled India to Saudi Arabia using a forged passport. The operative, originally from Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, married under an assumed identity in Jaipur, which aided his passport application. This revelation highlights significant loopholes in India's verification systems.

title: Dalal Street this week: US-Iran tensions, crude swings and Q4 earnings to drive markets
content: Dalal Street eyes US-Iran conflict, crude oil, and corporate earnings for direction. HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank reported Q4 results, with the former warning of West Asia conflict risks. Analysts anticipate continued focus on geopolitical developments and their impact on global markets and capital flows.

title: CBI's New AI Chatbot To Tackle Digital Arrests By Helping Verify Notices
content: The chatbot, 'Abhay', will be launched by Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant on the occasion of the 22nd D P Kohli Memorial Lecture organised by the CBI in honour of its founding director, officials said.

title: Israeli Army Probes Image Of Soldier Hitting Jesus Statue In Lebanon
content: The Israeli army said on Sunday that it was looking into the authenticity of an image circulating on social media that appears to show a soldier in south Lebanon hitting a statue of Jesus Christ with a hammer.

title: CBI Arrests Aviation Body Official In Rs 2.5 Lakh Bribery Case
content: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has arrested a senior official of the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) along with a private individual in connection with a Rs 2.5 lakh bribery case, officials said on Saturday.

title: Years On The Run, How Europe's Most Wanted Gang Leader Was Finally Tracked
content: Kinahan, 48, is expected to face charges in Ireland connected to a long-running feud between the Kinahan cartel and the Hutch gang.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Trump Announces 2nd Round Of Talks, Iran Hasn't Decided Yet, Says State Media
content: US-Iran War, Ceasefire, Peace Talks Updates: The war is in its eighth week and has sent oil prices surging because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: 7 Weeks On, Why Iran Hasn't Buried Ali Khamenei? Expert's 'Fear' Claim
content: Millions of people had turned up for his predecessor Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini's funeral in 1989. However, a similar funeral service couldn't be organised for Ali Khamenei.

title: Opinion: Women's Quota Takes Centrestage in Bengal As Voting Day Approaches
content: The debate intensified after Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused opposition parties of committing foeticide by opposing what he called a genuine constitutional effort to fast-track women's reservation in legislatures

title: Delhi Bets Big On Semiconductors, Eyes Chip Hub Status With New Policy
content: Delhi has entered the semiconductor race, with the government drafting a policy to build capacity in chip design, research and related segments, while aiming to create jobs and attract investment.

title: Probe Agency ED Summons I-PAC Director In Money Laundering Case
content: Rishi Raj Singh has been asked to depose on April 20 at the ED office in Delhi for questioning in the case related to alleged hawala transactions of funds received from political parties and other entities.

title: Maths Teacher In Punjab Charged For Molesting Colleague In Science Lab
content: According to the complaint, the woman joined the school in Bathinda as a science teacher in June 2023.

title: 'Won't Rest Until Accused Punished': Manipur Deputy Chief Minister Losii Dikho On Ambush
content: "It is heart-wrenching that this atrocity occurred just twelve hours after I visited Ukhrul district..." Manipur Deputy Chief Minister Losii Dikho said

title: High-Flying Iranian Woman Charged With Moving Arms Worth Millions For Tehran
content: Shamim Mafi, 44, was charged by the US Attorney's Office for the Central District of California with conducting arms transactions without legal authorisation

title: 'Trinamool Does Not Want Women Empowerment, Reservation': PM Modi In Bengal
content: PM Modiattacked the Mamata Banerjee-led party over its opposition to the bill, which sought to provide 33 per cent reservation forwomeninParliament and state assembliesbefore 2029.

title: Embattled UK PM Starmer To Face Grilling From MPs Over Mandelson Scandal
content: Embattled UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer will face lawmakers in parliament on Monday as he tries to quell anger over an unrelenting scandal involving long-time Jeffrey Epstein associate Peter Mandelson.

title: Indian Tanker Crosses Hormuz As 2 Vessels Forced To Turn Back After Firing
content: Crude oil tanker Desh Garima, with 31 Indian seafarers onboard, crossed the strategic waterway on April 18 and is expected to reach Mumbai on April 22.

title: Thousands Greet Amit Shah At Big Chennai Rally For Tamilisai Soundararajan
content: Thousands turned up to greet Amit Shah as he passed through Mylapore, the assembly constituency of BJP candidate Tamilisai Soundararajan and home to the historic Kapaleeshwarar temple

title: JD Vance To Lead US Team For Iran Talks In Pakistan: White House
content: US Vice President JD Vance will lead the US delegation for talks with Iran in Pakistan, a White House official said Sunday, after President Donald Trump indicated Vance would not make the trip.
